findings.evidence: LagrangianProd.lean:68 `@[simp] lemma isIsotropic_prod_iff`; Rescale.lean:121 `@[simp] lemma isIsotropic_rescale_iff`; LagrangianGraph.lean:107 has no attribute.
findings.issue: `isIsotropic_graph_iff` is the characteristic normal-form lemma reducing `(twistedProd ω₁ ω₂).IsIsotropic e.graph` to `IsSymplectomorphism ω₁ ω₂ e`, but is not `@[simp]`. Every analogous `_iff` characterization in the neighbouring files is `@[simp]` (`isIsotropic_prod_iff`, `isCoisotropic_prod_iff`, `isLagrangian_prod_iff` in LagrangianProd.lean; `isIsotropic_rescale_iff`, `isLagrangian_rescale_iff` in Rescale.lean). simp will discharge graph-isotropy goals inconsistently with prod/rescale goals.

findings.evidence: LagrangianProd.lean:118 `@[simp] lemma isLagrangian_prod_iff`; Rescale.lean:149 `@[simp] lemma isLagrangian_rescale_iff`; LagrangianGraph.lean:156 has no attribute.
findings.fix: Add `@[simp]` to `isLagrangian_graph_iff` (distinct LHS head from the isotropic lemma, so no loop).
findings.issue: `isLagrangian_graph_iff` (the top-level characterization the file advertises) is likewise not `@[simp]`, unlike `isLagrangian_prod_iff` and `isLagrangian_rescale_iff`.

summary: Minimal, well-characterized surface with hidden bodies and complete intro/elim iff lemmas. The characteristic `_iff` lemmas lack the `@[simp]` annotation that every parallel lemma in the sibling files carries, leaving a real automation gap.
